title: 第 65 節
en: Individual soul is Brahman, not vice versa, 154. Indra, the rainer, 35. Indriyagraya, subjugation of senses, Indriyas, five senses, 163, 415.
— sense, 173. Indu, the rain, 35, Inference, Anumana. 379.
— Smnti, 397. Instance, Udaharana, 432. Inward-turned thought, Pratyak- /cetana, 323.
Isvara exists phenomenally only, — the Lord, 188.
— or personal Lord, denial of, not in the original Samkhya, 230.
— a maker, a Sat-kara, 328. tsvaras, not many, 321.
— means isolation of the soul, 334. Kaivalya, 347, 359.
Zakrapravaitana, the turning of the wheel, 24.

Kakuda Katyayana, teacher mentioned in Buddhist annals, Kalanos (Kalyana) gymnosophist, Kalidasa, alludes to the logician Dignaga, 364. Kanada, 362, 372, 440. TTandrakanta Tarkalankara, author of Sanskrit treatise, 87 n. Kanishka, King, 85-106 A. D., 440. his Great Council, under Yasumitra and Purnaka, 440. Kan-ti, not a good Chinese scholar, S22.
— and Buddha, existence side by side of their systems, 316.
— did not borrow from Buddha, — -Sutras, age of, 220, 364.
